name: City of London Walk
title: Two thousand years in a square mile
note: Pictures for the big-picture opening: London's ancient core, the Great Fire, and the old-and-new city today.

items.caption: The Great Fire of 1666, which burned most of the medieval City in four days. London has been destroyed and rebuilt many times, and always comes back.
items.kidcaption: The Great Fire of 1666! It burned down most of the old city in just four days. But London always rebuilds itself, bigger than before.

items.caption: A surviving chunk of the Roman city wall. Under the modern square mile lie 2000 years of London, right down to the Roman streets.
items.kidcaption: A real piece of the wall the Romans built around London almost 2000 years ago. Bits of it still poke up between the modern buildings.

items.caption: The City today: Wren's dome of St Paul's among glass towers. The tiny ancient core is now one of the world's great financial centres, layered on Roman ground.
items.kidcaption: Look how the old round dome of St Paul's sits right next to shiny modern skyscrapers. Old and new, all squeezed together in the square mile.
